title: Carpinus oblongifolia
taxonomy: Carpinus oblongifolia is a species of tree in the family Betulaceae, commonly known as the birch family. This family is a group of flowering plants that are native to the Northern Hemisphere. The Betulaceae family is closely related to the Corylaceae family, and together they form the order Fagales. Carpinus oblongifolia is a deciduous tree that is native to East Asia, and it is often used in landscaping and forestry. Its classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the Fagales order.
uses: Carpinus oblongifolia has been traditionally used in folk medicine in certain regions, particularly in Eastern Asia. Its leaves and bark are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. In traditional medicine, Carpinus oblongifolia is used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. The plant's extracts are also reported to have antimicrobial and antifungal properties. Further research is needed to fully understand the potential medicinal applications and benefits of Carpinus oblongifolia.

intro: Carpinus oblongifolia, also known as the Chinese hornbeam or Chinese blue beech, is a deciduous tree species native to eastern Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, and has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive disorders. The bioactive constituents of Carpinus oblongifolia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Carpinus oblongifolia is often prepared as a decoction, tincture, or wine to treat a range of health conditions, and is also used in modern herbal medicine to support immune function and reduce inflammation.
